MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar May-08-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF ELIZABETH BLAND MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Edward M. Higginbotham, Esq., of Byrant Law Group, LLP, (415) 581-0885, for Plaintiff.
No other appearances.

At the court's query, counsel updates the court as to the status of the relationship between he and his client. The court grants Plaintiff Elizabeth Bland's Motion for Withdrawal of Attorney of Record. Counsel is to prepare and submit to the court an order on the Mandatory Judicial Council Form.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses, Without Objections, To Defendant Allison Adel Goldenbergs Form Interrogatories General (Set One); Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-28-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSES, WITHOUT OBJECTIONS, TO DEFENDANT ALLISON ADEL GOLDENBERG'S FORM INTERROGATORIES-GENERAL (SET ONE); REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS

Plaintiff Elizabeth Bland is present without counsel. The matter is not argued, and the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Allison Adel Goldenberg's motion to compel responses to her form interrogatories, set one, from plaintiff Elizabeth Bland is granted. Bland is ordered to provide objection-free, verified responses by June 1, 2026. (See Code Civ. Proc., sec. 2030.290.) Bland asks for a continuance of the motion, but the discovery responses have been due since January 20, 2026. (Horton Decl., para. 2.) Defendant seeks sanctions. The court has granted sanctions on a companion motion and finds that Bland's discovery position lacks substantial justification but concludes that the amount awarded on the first motion is sufficient and does not award additional sanctions on this separate motion. Defendant shall give notice of this order. Proposed order submitted. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses, Without Objections, To Defendant Manual Alberto Lopez Ramirezs Special Interrogatories (Set One); Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-28-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSES, WITHOUT OBJECTIONS, TO DEFENDANT MANUEL ALBERTO LOPEZ RAMIREZ'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES (SET ONE); REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS

Plaintiff Elizabeth Bland is present without counsel. The matter is not argued, and the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Manuel Alberto Lopez Ramirez's motion to compel responses to his special interrogatories, set one, from plaintiff Elizabeth Bland, and for sanctions, is granted. Bland is ordered to provide objection-free, verified responses by June 1, 2026. (See Code Civ. Proc., sec. 2030.290.) Bland asks for a continuance of the motion pending her counsel's motion to withdraw, but the discovery responses have been due since January 20, 2026. (Horton Decl., para. 2.) Defendant seeks sanctions. Code of Civil Procedure, section 2030.290, subdivision (c) provides that "the court shall impose a monetary sanction . . . against any party, person, or attorney who unsuccessfully makes or opposes a motion to compel a response to interrogatories . . . unless it finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust." Sanctions are appropriate on a discovery motion where the objecting party does not act with substantial justification in its discovery compliance, even where the party does not oppose the motion. (See Rule of Court 3.1348, subd. (a).) The court does not find substantial justification and orders Bland to pay sanctions of $1,372.50 to defendants Manuel Alberto Lopez Ramirez and Allison Adel Goldenberg within 30 days. Lopez Ramirez shall give notice of this order. Proposed order submitted. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)
